description_long: This is a stylized biplane with a vibrant yellow and purple color scheme, featuring flame-like patterns on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft has two stacked wings supported by black struts, and a wooden two-blade propeller mounted at the nose. The landing gear consists of two large wheels and a small tailwheel, indicating a classic taildragger configuration. The cockpit is open and positioned just behind the upper wing, with a visible pilot figure inside. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted in matching colors. The overall design is reminiscent of vintage stunt or racing planes, with exaggerated proportions and playful aesthetics.
description: A colorful, stylized biplane with yellow and purple flame patterns, open cockpit, and wooden propeller. Features classic double wings, taildragger landing gear, and a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, double wings, and landing gear with both wheels visible.
description_view_1: Angled side-top view highlighting the flame patterns on the wings and fuselage, as well as the open cockpit and tailwheel.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full extent of the flame patterns, both wings, and the open cockpit with the pilot figure.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side-top view emphasizing the tail section, wing struts, and the propeller from the side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
